differentials.name: cranial neuropathy (facial > acoustic > optic > trigeminal nerves) ← granulomatous infiltration + leptomeningeal fibrosis (50-75%) of rapid onset with spontaneous resolution
differentials.raw: cranial neuropathy (facial > acoustic > optic > trigeminal nerves) ← granulomatous infiltration + leptomeningeal fibrosis (50-75%) of rapid onset with spontaneous resolution

differentials.name: pituitary + hypothalamic dysfunction
differentials.raw: pituitary + hypothalamic dysfunction (eg, diabetes insipidus in 5-10%)

differentials.name: multiple sclerosislike symptoms
differentials.raw: multiple sclerosislike symptoms (from multifocal parenchymal involvement)

differentials.name: prompt improvement following therapy with steroids
differentials.raw: prompt improvement following therapy with steroids

differentials.name: spinal fluid: normal (in up to 30%) or nonspecific (↑ protein, ↑ leukocyte count, ↔ / ↓ glucose) Location:   leptomeninges, dura mater, subarachnoid space, peripheral nerves, brain parenchyma, ventricular system ◊  Affects meninges + cranial nerves more often than brain!
differentials.raw: spinal fluid: normal (in up to 30%) or nonspecific (↑ protein, ↑ leukocyte count, ↔ / ↓ glucose) Location:   leptomeninges, dura mater, subarachnoid space, peripheral nerves, brain parenchyma, ventricular system ◊  Affects meninges + cranial nerves more often than brain!

differentials.name: often associated with fever + arthralgia
differentials.raw: often associated with fever + arthralgia (= painful swollen adjacent joints) Onset:   typically associated with benign self-limiting acute sarcoidosis (eg, Löfgren syndrome)

differentials.name: Lupus pernio = indurated red-brown to violaceous (= bluish purple) papules + plaques
differentials.raw: Lupus pernio = indurated red-brown to violaceous (= bluish purple) papules + plaques

context: ◊  Portends a more chronic + adverse prognosis! ◊  Usually accompanied by cutaneous manifestations! @  Joint (up to 40%) Location:   knee, ankle, elbow, wrist √  inflammatory arthralgia @  Muscle √  increased T2 signal intensity of involved muscle √  sarcoid nodules with centrally low signal intensity on all sequences (= area of fibrosis) √  granulomas peripherally hyperintense on T2WI with contrast-enhancement on T1WI @  Bone (5-10%) Histo:   medullary granulomas infiltrate cortical bone → bone destruction / reactive sclerosis Location:   hands + feet (metaphyseal ends of distal + middle phal
